cards.back: A Nash equilibrium in a game where players randomize their strategies, making it difficult to predict their actions with certainty
A state where no player can improve their payoff without making another player worse off
The assumption that players make decisions based on their expected payoffs, taking into account the actions of other players
A concept in game theory where players randomize their strategies to achieve a Nash equilibrium
A concept in game theory where players randomize their strategies to achieve a Nash equilibrium, with the added constraint that the strategies must be correlated
